rationale: The citing paper uses the cited paper directly to analyze and interpret a key interaction effect, stating that it conducted a spotlight analysis following Spiller et al. (2013). It also reports a Johnson-Neyman threshold in the same follow-up probing context, consistent with the spotlight/floodlight guidance from that method paper. This is more than a background mention because the cited work informs the paper's empirical testing procedure for hypotheses.
relation_description: The citing study applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s moderated regression probing approach to decompose interaction effects in Study 2. The cited paper functions as methodological guidance for simple-effects testing and interpretation (including Johnson-Neyman style probing).
